rationale: Both correctly identify PR #4792 as the fix for the non-async-chunk (None, payload) starvation bug already folded into the 0.24 rebase (a560ed18), matching the thread's resolution exactly, and both give sound upgrade/workaround advice. X is slightly better grounded and more complete: it directly addresses specific lines from the reporter's own collect_env.py output (the NVFP4 patch note) to rule out red herrings, showing closer reading of the actual issue body, whereas Y cites a suspiciously named unit test ('test_sample_tokens_tail_only_prefix_cache_uses_staged_cpu_hidden_states') as 'explicitly validating this fix' — a claim that doesn't match its own name and reads as an unverified/likely fabricated citation. Both attribute the exact ground-truth code snippet with plausible but partly unverifiable file/line specifics.
